raw_consumer_statement:
on XX/XX/XXXX, XXXX  dream, homes ran a charge for {$4100.00} on my CITI card as a down payment for Windows for my home. I authorized a manual charger and the windows were to be delivered and installed within seven weeks. As requested by CITI CARDS, I wrote a letter of dispute to them identifying the charge made by XXXX XXXX XXXX as fraud/deceptive business practices because I never received the Windows. In addition, the salesman was not answering my calls or messages.CITI CARDS refunded me a full credit to my credit card statement for {$4100.00}. Two billing statements later they recharged my account with the amount that had been credited. I asked CITI to provide proof that the merchant had delivered and installed windows in my home. I mailed evidence to CITI CARDS, to include recent pictures of the same windows that were there when I purchased my home. I also mailed CITI CARDS the contract made by XXXX XXXX XXXX signed by the salesman, screenshots of text messages between myself and the salesman XXXX XXXX. 
CITI CARDS later mailed a request for me to request payment from XXXX dream homes in order for them to give me credit for the charge. I filed a police report for deceptive business practices. The detective assigned tracked down XXXX XXXX and asked for a resolution. As for the detective XXXX XXXX chose to remain silent. The case is now been turned over to the district attorneys office. 
I questioned CITI CARDS why they reversed tge charge on behalf of a fraudulent company. I have been with CITI CARDS for over15 years, have never been late on a payment and have kept making payments despite the fraud charge. I am a a XXXX  about to retire in seven weeks. CITI CARDS has not been fair. I am planning to pay my balance by XX/XX/XXXX, but I am not paying the {$4100.00}. I do not want this affecting my credit score. I have mailed CITI CARDS more than sufficient evidence proving that the aforementioned charge was in fact deceptive business practices from a non-existing company.
